statement: Judge-gym should split settings into a shared schema/default layer, a Convex-stored versioned operator policy layer that runs/windows snapshot, and runtime-local env/secret parsing for Convex and Temporal workers, rather than a single shared ENGINE_SETTINGS module imported everywhere.
rationale: ENGINE_SETTINGS is currently tightly coupled into Convex execution code and rate limiter init. A Temporal rewrite needs policy reproducibility without importing secrets across runtimes.
keyAssumptions: Runs/windows should remain reproducible, so they need a policy snapshot identifier
Workers can fetch policy snapshots via activities without bloating history or creating hidden nondeterminism
validationCriteria: A run/window started under policy X does not change behavior when policy Y becomes active later
No shared package reads process.env or imports Convex/Temporal runtime code
Operators can adjust policy for new runs without redeploying worker code
critiquePoints: If policy is fetched dynamically mid-run rather than snapshotted, reproducibility becomes fuzzy
questions: Which policy fields must be snapshotted vs allowed to drift for operational reasons (e.g. throttles)?
